vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting /etc/gshadow permissions to 0000 is a standard STIG hardening step. The file is read only by privileged processes, so changing its mode will not lock out SSH or console access and will not disrupt system services.
